response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished senior full-stack software engineer with strong web, backend, and developer-tooling experience, but the role demands deep hardware expertise — SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appears in the resume. Aside from brief C/C++ work at National Instruments and low-latency DLang services, there is no evidence of computer architecture, performance modeling for deep-learning workloads, or SIMD/MIMD experience. The experience-years requirement is met, but the domain mismatch is fundamental for a Principal-level hardware performance position.
strengths: 15+ years of software engineering experience, meeting the BS + 8 years bar
Low-latency systems work (async I/O, fibers, high-throughput services in DLang)
Recent exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and tooling at Google
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon, or computer-architecture experience
No deep-learning training/inference optimization or GPU workload analysis background
Career trajectory is web/full-stack, not hardware performance engineering at Principal depth

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator or DL workload optimization background
Strong general engineering seniority and systems fundamentals
